## [00:00 - 03:01]

Now, before we dive into launching campaigns, running testing and things like that, there are some fundamentals you need to get in place to really crack how to set up campaigns on meta, you know, Facebook and Instagram and things like that. So this section focuses on the things you need to get done first. So it's broken down into the sections here. Meta ads algorithm unleashed. So it's a few videos just to show you how the actual algorithm works to get you thinking about how to get low cost clicks and high quality users to your website. And once you've been through that, you'll understand what's driving the algorithm, what Facebook looking for. Then it's a case of making sure your account is set up as best as it can be, all the way from how your pixel is set up, your campaigns and things like that. Then we go into audience research. So a few techniques that I use to kind of support the research phase of, you know, how we use the BPM method. So by this point, I'm assuming that you've been through the BPM method. And there's some additional audience research tools within Facebook and meta, the platform that you can use. Then we go into ad building and creatives and creating kick ass ads. So this is a section around the creative process around creating ads specifically for the meta platform. The BPM method we talked broadly about creating ads that are magnetic and pull people out, engage, create engagement and create action. We now go deeper and one level down into looking at that for the meta ads platform, showing you how to write really, really good copy and select creatives that are really going to make a difference to help you stand out wherever you're advertising on the meta platform. Then we go into data mastery. This is like the numbers that I look at, the type of reports I set up, et cetera. Then the final section is CBO, campaign budget optimization, which I am still a big fan of. When was it, 2019? I think Facebook were going to make this mandatory so you could only set your budgets at the campaign level. You can now do it at campaign level and ad set. I do sometimes switch between the two, especially for testing. I think having ad set budgets is okay. Just bear in mind there's a balance between setting budgets at the ad set level and having to put the time into monitor and tracking, keep a close eye of what's happening, versus just trusting the algorithm. Now, if all you do is use campaign budgets, but you don't set up the mechanism to make that work, then you won't see a performance. We'll go through those sections. Once you've been through this, and by the way, I understand you're coming from many different levels. Some of you will be super experienced and you will have done all of these. Maybe you're starting out wherever you are, just go through the sections. You can watch the videos at 2X speed. You can skip the tracker on the video and go and see what the video is about. You may not need all of it.

## [03:01 - 03:56]

However, I encourage you to go through every video anyway. Not that you have to watch the whole thing, but make sure you've not missed anything because if you have, that can cause the whole system to not perform as optimally as you want it to. If you imagine going out to race a car on a track and you're so confident, but all you believe you need is a tweak and you're going to crush the lap time and you're going to win the race, what if you missed a configuration? What if you missed something that I use, my team use, other clients have used to really, really crush Facebook ads, Instagram ads and you've missed it because you've made the assumption that, hey, I know how to research audiences or I'm really good at data and yeah, I get CBO, just have a look at it. Go through the sections and then go into the testing optimization and scaling process and you'll be better for it.
